frameworks.name: representational similarity analysis
frameworks.one_line: Model-based RSA compares numerosity to other visual feature models across cortex

frameworks.name: neural representational geometry
frameworks.one_line: Low-dimensional embedding of BOLD patterns reveals coding structure

frameworks.name: two visual streams (dorsal/ventral)
frameworks.one_line: Numerosity coding compared across dorsal and ventral retinotopic and associative areas

core_question: How is visual numerosity encoded relative to other visual features across early visual and associative regions in both streams?
key_assumptions: Numerosity can be dissociated from item size, total area, field area, and density via stimulus control
Model-based RSA can separate competing visual-feature representations
Dimensionality reduction recovers meaningful coding geometry from BOLD patterns
manipulations: Varied item size, total item area, field area, and density
Compared early visual vs retinotopic vs non-retinotopic associative regions
Compared dorsal vs ventral stream regions
key_findings: Numerosity is represented independently of other visual properties in early visual areas
Numerosity representation is amplified in retinotopic and non-retinotopic associative regions across both streams
Early visual and ventral retinotopic areas show a one-dimensional representation of numerical rank
Associative dorsal and ventral regions show a curved geometry encoding rank and distance-to-endpoints
interpretations: Distinct neural coding schemes for numerosity operate across cortical regions
Numerosity is encoded across a broader cortical network beyond parietal cortex
